Expected result: We should only allow sending the number of characters that BE can accept. i.e while sending we should calculate the length of the final html converted text instead of the typed characters Actual result: None --- proposals.md --- # Proposals for issue 14268 ## Proposal 0 # Proposal ## Problem As the description says we don't count the actual sending characters, instead we just count the typed characters to determine a valid length. ## Solution 1 Count the html parsed characters and limit it to 60K as that is the limit that has been set in the backend/DB. --- ## Proposal 1 ## Proposal ### Please re-state the problem that we are trying to solve in this issue. The composer does not prevent sending comments containing large amounts of markdown that result in a `402 Maximum Size Exceeded` error from the API endpoints. ### What is the root cause of that problem? The comment length calculation does not account for the markdown conversion. ### What changes do you think we should make in order to solve the problem? I propose three changes to solve this issue: 1. Include the markdown transformation in the `getCommentLength` function. 2. Defer counting until there is a break in typing, since it's too expensive to perform for each key-press. 3. Abstract the calculation to a pure component, so calculating is only occurring once each time. A demonstration of the deferred counter can be viewed in the following video. The counter is normally hidden until the limit is reached, but was enabled permanently for this example: [Screencast from 2023-02-10 02:23:47 AM.webm](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1311325/218070115-a5b19680-72af-402b-bf0e-2a0dfdcf7d3a.webm) By adopting these changes, we are increasing the performance of the composer beyond where it's currently at, while at the same time solving this issue. The app is currently counting comment length four times per keypress due to re-renders of the composer components. See also [this screenshot](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1311325/218080532-ea6371b6-54b5-4355-ad7f-1b5eccbea726.png) demonstrating the accurate character count for the OP of this issue. ### Additional clarifications from the same user in later comments #### Comment at https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/14268#issuecomment-1430846150 > It actually increases typing performance for long messages. We're currently encoding the message four times for every keypress. By debouncing the calculation until there is a 500ms break in typing, we are eliminating any process blocking while typing characters. > > The plain-text message will still contain the markdown syntax. The user would have no indication why their message was not formatted as expected. In a professional context this could reflect poorly on the sender of the message. > > My solution also takes into account the 10,000 character plain-text conversion by abstracting the same function. No markdown conversions would be happening in the counter beyond this amount either. > > I disagree. The addition of a server limit constant means it would need to be updated along with other code changes. It's a big part of the reason why we did not want to add such a constant for the other issue. The reality is that updating the value will be overlooked and this issue will re-appear. #### Comment at https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/14268#issuecomment-1430873635 > We are not actually modifying what they're sending, we're infact sending the text as it is if it's greater than 10K. > > It's better to let users control what they are sending rather than modifying their message without their knowledge. > > The conversion to plain-text at 10,000 characters is because the server has trouble processing that amount of markdown, and a workaround like that should be avoided if possible. > > I disagree. The addition of a server limit constant means it would need to be updated along with other code changes. #### Comment at https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/14268#issuecomment-1432848013 > Thank you @francoislm > > I can demonstrate the performance enhancement with two videos. > > As you can see, the test above completely locks up my browser while performing key events due to the thousands of times the `getCommentLength` function is being invoked. The composer has noticeable lag, even with regular typing. > > In the second video, we see that the optimization greatly improves performance. #### Comment at https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/14268#issuecomment-1435015513 > It was already decided to [encode the comment](https://github.com/redstar504/ExpensifyApp/blob/3850a7c219328ea0ba1c9f9422ef8591ea992cae/src/libs/ReportUtils.js#L1391) to determine the comment length in the other issue. > > I will report back with some benchmarks so we can get a better idea of how long the markdown conversion will take with the debouncing optimization. #### Comment at https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/14268#issuecomment-1435294395 > I benchmarked the string from OP at around 250ms. It was barely noticeable while adding or deleting text as you can see in the video below. # Proposal review (SWE manager tasks)

You are acting as the engineering manager for the Expensify App open-source project.
Each directory under `tasks/` contains one real GitHub issue and the formal proposals
contributors submitted to fix it:

- `tasks/<issue>/issue.md` — the bug report (steps, expected/actual results)
- `tasks/<issue>/proposals.md` — the competing proposals, each with a numeric id

For every task, review the issue and all proposals, and decide which single proposal
should be accepted and implemented. Judge root-cause correctness, scope, and risk the
way a maintainer would; do not pick a proposal just because it is longest or first.

## Deliverable

Write your decisions to `manager_decisions.json` at the repository root, in exactly
this format (one entry per issue directory name):

```json
{
  "14268": { "selected_proposal_id": 0 },
  "15193": { "selected_proposal_id": 0 },
  "25901": { "selected_proposal_id": 0 }
}
```

The `selected_proposal_id` values must be the numeric ids from `proposals.md`.

### Please re-state the problem that we are trying to solve in this issue.
Message turns bold temporarily when enclosed with a `#` followed by ``` or `.

### What is the root cause of that problem?
The frontend parses the expression in a different way then the backend. Frontend encloses the `
` tag inside the `h1` tag while the backend does not enclose the `pre` tag inside `h1`.

### What changes do you think we should make in order to solve the problem?

We can change the ExpensiMark `#` parsing rule such that it ignores adding `h1` if `pre` or `code` tag is present.
The rule exists here:
https://github.com/Expensify/expensify-common/blob/4f61e5529c89936bfb72b6cee53250990c16f188/lib/ExpensiMark.js#L166

The new regex would look like the following:
```
/^#(?!#) +(?!<(pre|code)>)(.+)$\s*/gm
```

The [replacement part](https://github.com/Expensify/expensify-common/blob/4f61e5529c89936bfb72b6cee53250990c16f188/lib/ExpensiMark.js#L167) of this regex needs to be changed as well:

```
replacement: '
$2
',
```

This works well after the fix.

### Please re-state the problem that we are trying to solve in this issue.
Bold text appears temporarily, then reverts to normal text, when sending ``# ```code``` ``.

### What is the root cause of that problem?
The problem is related to the way the frontend and backend parse expressions, resulting in differences between the two. Specifically, the frontend sends `
code
`, while the backend replies with `
code
`.

### What changes do you think we should make in order to solve the problem?
To address the root cause of the problem, I recommend making the following changes:

- Adjust the CSS for the "pre" class to prevent it from inheriting font weight and size from its parent.
- Change the CSS for the "h1" class to use lineHeight instead of marginBottom, to take into consideration that the title might be empty.

I believe that the changes outlined above are the most effective and simple way to address the problem.

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2527288/219359884-b7ea7b53-0c85-4e17-8d56-9f55c2e6a86a.mov

None

--- Additional clarifications from comment #1441316394 (also by moezh) ---

"Here's a more detailed explanation of the changes i'll make to the CSS (src/styles/styles.js) for the "pre" and "h1" classes, in order to solve the problem:

For the "pre" class, I'll add following properties:
- Add fontWeight, fontSize, and lineHeight: This ensures that the "pre" element doesn't inherit these properties from its parent, and instead uses the default properties.

For the "h1" class, I'll change following properties:

system: <general_instruct>
Call tools or skills to finish the task.
</general_instruct>
<response_instruct>
Before ending this run, you MUST determine whether a response needs to be sent via channel, checking the following conditions:
1. Has the user asked you a question waiting for your answer?
2. Is there any error or important information that needs to be sent to the user immediately?
3. If it is a casual chat, does the conversation need to be continued?

**IMPORTANT:** Your plain/direct reply in this chat will be ignored.
**Therefore, you MUST send messages via channel using the correct skill if a response is needed.**

When responding to a channel message, you MUST:
1. Identify the channel from the message metadata (e.g., `$telegram`, `$discord`)
2. Send your message as instructed by the channel skill (e.g., `telegram` skill for `$telegram` channel)
</response_instruct>
<context_contract>
Excessively long context may cause model call failures. In this case, you MAY use tape.info to retrieve the token usage and you SHOULD use tape.handoff tool to shorten the retrieved history.
</context_contract>
